title: How to Prepare for a Starlink Installation
excerpt: A simple checklist for choosing the installation point, preparing power, and improving WiFi coverage before the technicians arrive.
content:
## Choose a clear installation area
Starlink needs a wide, unobstructed view of the sky. Trees, walls, tall buildings, and nearby structures can interrupt the connection. Identify a roof, pole, or open area with the clearest possible view.

## Confirm safe access
Make sure the installer can safely reach the proposed mounting point. Tell the team in advance when special ladders, long cable routes, or elevated structures may be involved.

## Prepare reliable power
The Starlink router and dish need stable power. Decide where the equipment will connect and consider surge protection or backup power when your area experiences outages.

## Think about WiFi coverage
The position of the router affects signal strength inside the building. Large homes, double-storey buildings, offices, and properties with thick walls may need mesh units or extra access points.

## Keep your contact and location details ready
Share your exact location, preferred installation date, and the main areas that need internet coverage. This helps the installation team prepare the correct equipment and plan the work.

title: Five Ways CCTV Helps Protect a Small Business
excerpt: CCTV is more than recorded footage. A well-planned system improves visibility, accountability, incident response, and peace of mind.
content:
## Visible deterrence
Clearly positioned cameras can discourage theft, vandalism, and unauthorised access before an incident happens.

## Better incident review
Recorded footage helps you understand what happened, when it happened, and which area was affected. Good camera placement is just as important as image quality.

## Remote monitoring
Authorised users can view cameras from a phone or computer when the system is correctly connected and secured. This is useful for checking a shop, office, warehouse, or entrance while away.

## Staff and customer safety
Cameras can help identify unsafe situations, improve emergency response, and provide evidence when disputes occur.

## Operational visibility
Business owners can also use CCTV to review queues, receiving areas, opening and closing procedures, and high-traffic points.

A security assessment should consider entrances, blind spots, lighting, recording duration, storage capacity, and remote access—not only the number of cameras.

title: What Every Business Website Should Include
excerpt: The essential pages and features that help visitors understand your business, trust your brand, and contact you quickly.
content:
## A clear explanation of what you do
Visitors should understand your main service within a few seconds. Use a direct headline, a short supporting statement, and a visible call to action.

## Separate service pages
Each major service deserves its own page. This gives clients enough information and makes it easier to share a direct link for the exact service they need.

## Trust information
Include real contact details, your location or service area, project examples, client reviews where available, and a professional business identity.

## Mobile-friendly design
Many clients will visit from a phone. Text, buttons, forms, menus, and images should work properly on small screens.

## Fast contact options
Provide phone, email, WhatsApp, and a simple enquiry form. Do not make visitors search for a way to reach you.

## Useful information
A blog or resources section lets you answer common questions, educate clients, and keep the site active with new information.
